scenarios.name: Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Order coffee and cafe drinks easily
scenarios.tip: Tea and coffee stops move quickly, so point if needed and say sugar or takeaway early.

scenarios.name: Street Food / Restaurant
scenarios.tip: Keep the order short and confirm takeaway, bottled water, or spice level before they plate it.

scenarios.name: Ride App / Taxi
scenarios.tip: Show the map first, then confirm the stop or route if traffic or pickup points get confusing.

scenarios.name: Asking the Price
scenarios.tip: Ask the price before agreeing; some bazaars allow negotiation, but many everyday shops do not.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.tip: Merhaba, tesekkur ederim, and pardon or affedersiniz carry a lot of everyday goodwill.

scenarios.name: Convenience Store
scenarios.tip: These shops are useful for water, tissues, chargers, receipts, and quick card-or-cash questions.

scenarios.name: Hotel / Hostel
scenarios.tip: Have your passport and booking ready, and ask early about checkout time or room problems.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.tip: Ask about the metro, tram, ferry, or the nearest landmark and keep the map open while they answer.

scenarios.name: Simple Problems
scenarios.tip: Use a short repair phrase first, then show the booking, map, medicine box, or screenshot.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Short friendly chat usually stays simple: where you are from, whether it is your first visit, and compliments about food.

notes: Shared traveler baseline scaffold for Turkish, now reordered around first-wave traveler utility. Fill translation, pronunciation, Turkish-character search handling, and audio posture before runtime wiring.
